method_atom: A systematic construction of the complete action and local-symmetry transformation rules for a gauge field theory coupled to an arbitrary number of distinct matter sectors, in which a chosen subgroup of the theory's internal and geometric (isometry) symmetries is promoted to a local, gauged symmetry. The resulting theory is then analyzed for its symmetry-preserving stable ground states and for protected, partly-symmetric localized solutions that separate distinct regions of the ground state, providing a mechanism by which a lower-dimensional sub-region can support its own effective dynamics.
object_atom: Five-dimensional N=2 gauged supergravity coupled to arbitrary numbers of vector, tensor, and hyper-multiplets, with gauging of the SU(2)_R R-symmetry and a subgroup of the scalar-manifold isometries; its anti-de Sitter vacua, BPS solutions, the supersymmetric Randall-Sundrum brane-world scenario, and its link to type IIB string theory on the T^{11} manifold.
brief_reason: What carries across is the general recipe for building a matter-coupled locally-symmetric gauge theory, gauging an internal-symmetry subgroup, and classifying its stable vacua and protected domain-separating solutions; what is domain-specific is the 5D supergravity multiplet content, R-symmetry, AdS/BPS/brane-world physics, and string-theory embedding.
